compiled_state.author: Claude

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (--ref HEAD). Advisory triage: added test_show_json_unclaimed_ticket_has_null_session (T6/E5 unclaimed=null contract). Justified/no-op: E1 types.py regen is covered by test_public_types.py::test_types_module_is_not_stale; G1G2 the 5 other TicketStateOut MCP tools + test_schema_outputs::test_ticket_state_llm already validate against the shared schema (which carries claimed_session/csn) — no changes needed. Not re-editing to preserve the fresh attestation.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
S2 (68ef) folds `claimed_session` into compiled state, but a folded state key is only
useful if it is DISCOVERABLE on the read surface and DURABLE across compaction. This story
makes `claimed_session` a first-class, documented read field and proves it survives
snapshot/compaction round-trips (incl. the pre-feature snapshot case).

## What
- **MCP outputSchema (`src/rebar/_mcp_models.py`).** Add `claimed_session: str | None = None`
  to `TicketStateOut` so `show_ticket`'s advertised outputSchema enumerates it (the
  compiled-state schema `ticket_state.schema.json` and the generated `types.py` already carry
  it from S2).
- **LLM read format (`src/rebar/reducer/llm_format.py` + `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`).**
  Add `"claimed_session": "csn"` to `KEY_MAP` (and the co-located `Key mapping:` docstring
  table) so `to_llm` renders the value under the compact key `csn` when set (a `None` value is
  omitted, per the existing convention). Enumerate the new alias in the authoritative LLM
  output schema `src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state_llm.schema.json` — its `description` key-map
  list AND its `properties` block — and regenerate `src/rebar/types.py`.
- **Compaction safety (`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py`).** NO active `process_snapshot`
  guard is needed (unlike `managed_refs` which is seeded, or `attestations` which is folded):
  a POST-feature snapshot carries `claimed_session` in `compiled_state` and the generic
  restore loop restores it verbatim; a PRE-feature snapshot simply lacks the key, leaving the
  `make_initial_state` seed (`None`) intact. This story adds a compaction round-trip
  regression test pinning BOTH directions (add only a short clarifying comment at the restore
  loop — no code guard).
- **`show` JSON.** `rebar show` returns the compiled-state dict, which ALREADY includes
  `claimed_session` (it is a state key) — no separate JSON-rendering change is needed; a test
  asserts its presence for a claimed ticket.
- **Docs (`docs/event-schema.md`).** Document `claimed_session`: the STATUS
  `data["session"]` → `state["claimed_session"]` storage, the ordered resolver precedence
  (cross-ref S1 / `docs/config.md`), the fork-winner gating, and the compaction / forward-compat
  behavior (older clones ignore the additive key; pre-feature snapshots default to `None`).

## Scope
- IN: the MCP model field, the `to_llm` `KEY_MAP` entry + docstring + `ticket_state_llm.schema.json`
  (`csn`) + `types.py` regen, a compaction round-trip regression test (both directions, no code
  guard), a `show`-JSON presence test, and the `docs/event-schema.md` section.
- OUT: the `list --session=<id>` filter — DEFERRED to a follow-up ticket (it expands the
  CLI + MCP + `apply_ticket_filters` + `match_predicate` arg surface for optional value, out
  of proportion to this story). Also OUT: multi-harness fields (S5 / c557), capture hooks
  (S4 / S6). The record/fold path is S2 (68ef).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `show <id>` JSON includes `claimed_session` for a claimed ticket. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py -k show_json -q`.
- [ ] MCP `show_ticket` advertises `claimed_session` in its outputSchema. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py -k outputschema -q` asserts
      `"claimed_session" in TicketStateOut.model_json_schema()["properties"]`.
- [ ] `to_llm` renders the session under `csn` when set and omits it when `None`. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py -k to_llm -q`.
- [ ] `csn` is enumerated in `ticket_state_llm.schema.json` (properties + description key-map).
      Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py -k llm_schema -q`.
- [ ] Compaction round-trip preserves `claimed_session` (post-feature snapshot), AND a
      pre-feature snapshot whose `compiled_state` lacks the key restores to `None` (from the
      `make_initial_state` seed) without error. Proof:
      `pytest tests/unit/test_claimed_session_read.py -k compaction -q` covers both directions.
- [ ] `docs/event-schema.md` documents `claimed_session` in a DEDICATED section (storage
      `data["session"]` -> `claimed_session`, precedence cross-ref, compaction/forward-compat).
      Proof: a doc-content test that anchors on the section header
      "## Session provenance (`claimed_session`)" and asserts `claimed_session`, `data["session"]`,
      and "compaction" appear WITHIN that section (not merely somewhere in the file).

## Depends on
- S2 (68ef) — the record/fold path; landed in this stack.
compiled_state.title: Expose claimed_session on the read surface + compaction-safety guarantee
